summary: The provided literature does not identify "plate clearing" as a clinically meaningful factor linked to obesity; instead, established risks include genetic predisposition, dietary patterns, physical inactivity, and socioeconomic status—all unrelated to dish removal behaviors at mealtimes. Therefore, plate clearing lacks empirical support as an independent contributor to bodyweight regulation or disease development among adults.
